name: ERPSMART Builder Agent Safety Boundaries
ai_builder_agent_can: run the end-to-end smoke verifier with temporary DB record cleanup
guide the Builder Studio demo journey with read, save, validate, and preview only
propose archive, disable, or delete-draft rules for a future approved lifecycle task
archive unpublished Builder definitions when explicitly requested
restore archived Builder definitions when explicitly requested
delete unpublished Builder draft/control-plane records when explicitly requested
ai_builder_agent_cannot: treat publish readiness as actual publish
treat publish readiness diagnostic artifact as a runtime module
copy publish dry-run artifacts into runtime paths
copy publish candidate snapshots into runtime paths
use MCP to bypass permissions, approval, or audit
hide existing modules without an explicitly approved implementation flow
remove runtime capabilities without dependency analysis and approved lifecycle implementation
edit generated runtime module files directly
edit licensing or updater code directly
claim publish exists in Builder Studio MVP
treat preview artifacts as source of truth
treat vector indexes as source of truth

notes: Lifecycle planning is allowed because it produces docs and contracts only.
Definition lifecycle MVP allows archive, restore, and delete-draft for unpublished Builder control-plane records only when explicitly requested.
Publish readiness analysis is read-only and must not be treated as actual publish.
Publish readiness diagnostic artifact is derived storage and must not be treated as a runtime module or source of truth.
Publish dry-run artifacts are sandbox review artifacts and must not be copied into runtime paths.
Dry-run review is informational only; AI must not approve publish.
Publish candidate snapshots are derived storage artifacts and must not be copied into runtime paths.
Human approval request persistence exists; AI must not autonomously request, approve, reject, or revoke approval.
Approval request persistence exists, but AI must not autonomously approve, reject, revoke, or treat approval as publish.
Approved candidate preflight eligibility is not publish and must not be treated as a published module.
Publish execution records are control-plane preparation records only; AI may summarize them but must not autonomously create them.
Publish execution architecture is planning only; AI may summarize it but must not execute publish, acquire locks, write runtime files, or execute rollback.
RAG is the knowledge layer, Tool Registry is the action layer, and MCP is a future adapter that must not bypass permissions, approvals, or audit.
Lifecycle execution requires a separate implementation task with dependency checks, backups, data retention rules, rollback rules, permissions, and verifier coverage.

allowed: render preview into a uniquely named storage/app/module-builder-preview smoke directory
assert preview output contains Real runtime writes performed: 0
cleanup temporary DB records and unique smoke preview artifacts

business_operations_agent_separation.purpose: Business Operations Agent works with runtime business data after modules exist.
can: use permissioned runtime business APIs later
create records through approved tools later
update records through approved tools later
analyze operational data through approved tools later
cannot: modify Builder schema unless explicitly authorized
modify module definitions unless explicitly authorized

source_of_truth_rules.builder_definitions: Database definitions and immutable version snapshots are source of truth for draft Builder intent.
source_of_truth_rules.published_runtime: Published runtime modules and migrations will become runtime truth only after a future publish workflow.
source_of_truth_rules.preview_files: Generated preview files are derived artifacts and are not source of truth.
source_of_truth_rules.rag_docs: RAG docs guide agents but are not runtime truth.
source_of_truth_rules.vector_index: Vector indexes are derived and rebuildable.
